scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU

O.S. No. _____ of 2024

BETWEEN:

ANITA SHARMA,
Aged 52 years,
Residing at Indiranagar,
Bengaluru.

... PLAINTIFF

AND:

RAJESH REDDY,
Aged 60 years,
Residing at HSR Layout,
Bengaluru.

... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF AGREEMENT TO SALE

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff submits that this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try and entertain this suit as the property which is the subject matter of the suit is situated within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Koramangala, Bengaluru.

2. The Plaintiff further submits that the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court as the Agreement to Sell was executed in Bengaluru and the Defendant resides at HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

FACTS OF THE CASE

3. The Plaintiff states that she entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant dated 10.01.2024 for the purchase of a residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs.85,00,000/-.

4. The Plaintiff states that on the date of the agreement i.e., 10.01.2024, she paid an advance amount of Rs.25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654.

5. The Plaintiff states that as per the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the balance consideration of Rs.60,00,000/- was to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against execution of the sale deed.

6. The Plaintiff states that on 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter refusing to execute the sale deed, claiming he received a higher offer.

7. The Plaintiff states that she issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the sale deed.

8. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant refused to comply with the legal notice.

READINESS AND WILLINGNESS

9. The Plaintiff submits that she has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract as required under Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

10. The Plaintiff submits that she is ready and willing to pay the balance consideration of Rs.60,00,000/- as per the terms of the Agreement to Sell upon execution of the sale deed.

LEGAL BASIS

11. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which provides for specific performance of contracts concerning immovable property, applicable as the subject matter is a residential flat.

12. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which mandates that specific performance cannot be enforced in favour of a person who has not performed or has not been ready and willing to perform the essential terms of the contract, which the Plaintiff has fulfilled.

13. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which allows the court to award compensation in lieu of or in addition to specific performance.

14. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which empowers the court to grant possession or partition or refund of earnest money as the case may require.

15. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 10 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which establishes that all agreements are contracts if they are made by the free consent of parties competent to contract, for a lawful consideration and with a lawful object.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The Plaintiff submits that the cause of action arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant refused to execute the sale deed despite the Plaintiff's readiness and willingness.

LIMITATION

17. The Plaintiff submits that the suit is within limitation as per Article 54 of the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years from the date fixed for performance or when the plaintiff has notice that performance is refused.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The Plaintiff values the suit for the purpose of court fee and jurisdiction at Rs.85,00,000/- being the total consideration amount.

19. The Plaintiff pays a court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} on the plaint as per the Karnataka Court-Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

PRAYER

20. The Plaintiff therefore prays for a judgment and decree of this Hon'ble Court:

(a) Directing the Defendant to specifically perform the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 and execute the sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;

(b) Granting possession of the suit schedule property to the Plaintiff upon execution of the sale deed;

(c) Awarding compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance;

(d) Awarding costs of the suit; and

(e) Granting such other reliefs as this Hon'ble Court deems fit in the circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTY

21. The suit schedule property is described as follows:
Residential Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

22. The Plaintiff lists the following documents in support of the suit:
23. Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 (Original)
24. RTGS Receipt for Rs.25,00,000/- (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)
25. Defendant's Refusal Letter dated 05.06.2024
26. Legal Notice dated 20.06.2024 with Postal Receipt

VERIFICATION

27. I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 26 are true to my knowledge and based on the records in my possession.

Verified at Bengaluru on this ___ day of ___ 2024.

_______________________

PLAINTIFF

Counsel for Plaintiff
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Bengaluru
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION) AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} of 2024

BETWEEN:

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act,
Having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years. ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT

The Plaintiff above named submits as follows:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a company registered under the Companies Act having its registered office at Bengaluru. The Defendant is a limited liability partnership having its office at Bengaluru. Both parties are within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

2. The subject matter of the suit is a commercial dispute arising out of a Software Development Agreement executed between the parties. The specified value of the suit is Rs. 32,00,000/-, which exceeds the minimum threshold as required under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. Hence, this Hon'ble Commercial Court has the pecuniary and subject matter jurisdiction to try this suit.

FACTS OF THE CASE

3. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for development of an ERP system for a contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-.

4. On 01.02.2024, pursuant to the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as first milestone payment to the Defendant via NEFT bearing UTR No. ICIC20240201456789.

5. As per the terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

6. The Defendant failed to deliver any module by the deadline of 01.05.2024.

7. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without returning the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid by the Plaintiff, as evidenced by email correspondence.

8. Due to the non-delivery and abandonment of the project, the Plaintiff suffered additional loss of Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations.

9. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant demanding refund and damages.

10. The Defendant has failed to comply with the legal notice or refund the amount, necessitating the present suit.

LEGAL BASIS

11. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 37 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which binds parties to perform their respective promises under the Agreement unless excused under the provisions of the Act.

12. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 39 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant refused to perform their promise wholly, entitling the Plaintiff to put an end to the contract.

13. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 55 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant failed to perform the promise within the fixed time stipulated in the Agreement.

14. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which entitles the Plaintiff to compensation for any loss or damage caused by the breach of contract.

15. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 74 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which entitles the Plaintiff to reasonable compensation for the breach stipulated in the contract.

16. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, which entitles the Plaintiff to interest on the principal amount from the date of cause of action till realization.

17. {{MEDIATION_COMPLIANCE_STATUS}}

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project and failed to perform their contractual obligations.

LIMITATION

19. The suit is filed within three years from the date of cause of action as per Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The suit is within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee. The Plaintiff pays ad valorem court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} on the claim amount.

PRAYER

21. The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree against the Defendant for:

(a) Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- being the advance payment made under the Agreement;

(b) Recovery of Rs. 12,00,000/- being consequential damages for loss of operations;

(c) Interest at 18% per annum on the total amount from 15.06.2024 till realization;

(d) Cost of the suit; and

(e) Any other relief this Hon'ble Court deems fit to grant in the interests of justice.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

22. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:

(a) Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024;

(b) NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789);

(c) Email correspondence showing abandonment;

(d) Loss assessment report by auditor;

(e) Legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

VERIFICATION

23. I, Priya Menon, Director of TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 22 of the plaint are true to my knowledge and based on the records of the company.

24. I further verify that this statement is true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief and no part of it is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom, as required under Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 and Order VI Rule 15A of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 read with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

Verified at Bengaluru on this the {{DAY}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

PLACE: BENGALURU

PLAINTIFF

TECHSOFT SOLUTIONS PVT LTD
Through its Director

PRIYA MENON

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
{{BAR_COUNCIL_NUMBER}}
Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
